title: calochortus palmeri var. dunnii
intro: Calochortus palmeri var. dunnii, also known as the Palmer's mariposa lily, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its tubers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly by the indigenous peoples, for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and may also have antiseptic and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the tubers of Calochortus palmeri var. dunnii are typically dried and crushed or made into a tincture or tea, though further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
